notes:
Customers are seeing great results with solutions fueled by Azure Databases

BMW:
Before 2018, BMW Group’s development fleet relied on manual data transfer and on-premises data processing, significantly slowing vehicle development and prototype cycles.

The company created a mobile data recorder (MDR) solution, placing an IoT device in each development car to transmit data over a cellular connection to an Azure cloud platform, where Azure AI and data solutions facilitate efficient data analysis.

The MDR system and copilot fueled by Azure significantly enhances data accessibility, speeds prototypes and troubleshooting, and elevates development quality.

https://www.microsoft.com/en/customers/story/19769-bmw-ag-azure-app-service


NFL:
The NFL, with Microsoft and SOUTHWORKS, enhanced draft-day decisions using a conversational AI assistant in the Combine app. Powered by Azure OpenAI, Cosmos DB, and Container Apps, it delivers real-time insights on 300+ athletes—transforming manual analysis into instant intelligence through natural language queries for scouts and coaches.

https://www.microsoft.com/en/customers/story/23954-nfl-combine-azure-ai-foundry 


Medigold Health:
Facing the challenge of increasing client demand and limited clinician resources, Medigold Health wanted to find ways to reduce administrative burdens on clinicians so they can focus more on patient care—and have better work-life balance.

With Microsoft Azure as a foundation, the Medigold team migrated their applications and use Azure SQL Database for data storage. Azure SQL Database is the main persistence layer for storing AI-generated responses and making them accessible to clinicians through Medigold's internal application.
 
Automated processes, especially with report generation, are saving clinicians time, leading to a 58% rise in clinician retention and greater job satisfaction.

https://www.microsoft.com/en/customers/story/19709-medigold-health-consultancy-azure 

Thomson Reuters:
… and Thomsom Reuters migrated 18K databases across 500+ TB of data to Azure Managed SQL with full SQL Server compatibility—no major code changes needed.

The move boosted performance for 7K firms and 70K users, while also supporting the company’s global operations with high availability and built-in disaster recovery capabilities across regions.


Thomson Reuters transforms tax prep for 7K businesses with Azure SQL Managed Instance | Microsoft Customer Stories
https://www.microsoft.com/en/customers/story/24427-thomson-reuters-azure
